title: Comforting Crisp Ethiopian Chicken Doro Wot
description: A smoky and comforting Ethiopian dish featuring balsamic vinegar and a soffritto-base, cooked to crispy perfection in an iron teppan, this doro wot is a kosher-friendly delight.
instructions: Heat the iron teppan over medium-high heat.
Add the olive oil and swirl it around to coat the bottom.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is translucent and starting to caramelize.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger and cook for 1 minute.
Add the chicken thighs and cook until they are browned on all sides.
Add the berbere spice and cook for 1 minute.
Add the balsamic vinegar and bring to a simmer.
Reduce the heat to medium-low and cook until the chicken is cooked through and the sauce is thick and syrupy.
Season with salt and black pepper to taste.
Serve the chicken and sauce hot, garnished with chopped fresh herbs if desired.
